title: Bannerghatta National Park
tagline: Bannerghatta National Park — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Bannerghatta National Park is a national park in India, located In Bengaluru and Ramanagara District, Karnataka.
overview.description: Bannerghatta National Park is a national park in India, located In Bengaluru and Ramanagara District, Karnataka. It was founded in 1970 and declared as a national park in 1974. In 2002, a small portion of the park became a zoological garden, the Bannerghatta Biological Park. There are ancient temples in the park for worship and it is a destination for trekking and hiking.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: AMC Institutions
topPlaces.description: AMC Group of Institutions are educational institutions in Bangalore, India. The group primarily operate higher education institutions like AMC College of Hotel Management, AMC Engineering College, Advanced Business Management College, AMC PU College, its spin-off branch: AMC College (Jayanagar) and City College of Engineering.

topPlaces.name: Thottikallu
topPlaces.description: Thottikallu is a place near Bangalore off the Kanakapura road in the Bangalore Urban district. It is famous for a falls called Thottikallu falls, more popularly known as TK falls.

topPlaces.name: Koppa gate
topPlaces.description: Koppa Gate is a major junction on Bannerghatta Road, the main route through southern Bengaluru, in Karnataka, India. It is located where Begur-Koppa road crosses perpendicular at the boundary of Koppa and Harapanahalli Villages in Jigani in Anekal taluk.

topPlaces.name: Gulakamale inscriptions and hero stones
topPlaces.description: Gulakamale is located in Bangalore South taluk of Bangalore district in Karnataka, India. Two inscriptions are found in Gulakamale of which one is a donation inscription, and the other, a Mahasati herostone, a typology of herostones dedicated to women who self-sacrificed in sati.

topPlaces.name: Gulakamale
topPlaces.description: Gulakamale is a village in Bangalore district, Karnataka, India, near Kaggalipura on Bangalore – Kanakapura Road. The population of the village is 2,293 which made up of 1,131 males and 1,162 females.

topPlaces.name: Gottigere
topPlaces.description: Gottigere is a suburb in the southern periphery of Bangalore city, along Bannerghatta Road in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is surrounded by the famous NICE Road and smaller areas like Basavanapura and Kalena Agrahara.

topPlaces.name: Anjanapura inscriptions and hero stones
topPlaces.description: It is a Kannada inscription paleographically dated to the 15th century CE, discovered by the Mythic Society Bengaluru Inscriptions 3D Digital Conservation Project team in April 2022 in the Anjaneya Temple, Anjanapura. Much information cannot be contextualized as the text is effaced but appears to be a donatory inscription.

topPlaces.name: Anjanapura
topPlaces.description: Anjanapura Township, officially Lal Bahadur Shastri Nagara is a newly developing neighborhood located in the southern part of the city of Bengaluru. The area is located to the east of Kanakapura Road, north of NICE Road and west of Bannerghatta Road.

items.note: AMC Group of Institutions are educational institutions in Bangalore, India. faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bannerghatta National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bannerghatta National Park is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bannerghatta National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (46 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (20 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bannerghatta National Park?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bannerghatta National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bannerghatta National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Bannerghatta National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bannerghatta National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are AMC Institutions, Thottikallu, Koppa gate, Gulakamale inscriptions and hero stones, Gulakamale.
